skill.name: file-organizer
skill.description: Organizes local folders by analyzing file types, duplicate candidates, dates, and project context before proposing safe cleanup steps.
skill.summary: Plan and perform safer local file cleanup with reviewable organization steps.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell and blocker patterns, but most are Markdown examples or benign text in SKILL.md rather than bundled executable code. The real risk is that the skill instructs an AI agent to inspect broad local directories and run file-moving or duplicate-detection commands, so publication should include a filesystem safety warning.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Modification Workflow Requires User Review
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to analyze local folders, create directories, move files, rename files, and handle duplicate deletion decisions. These are legitimate file-organization actions, but they can cause data loss or privacy exposure if run too broadly or without clear confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented behavior directly includes file moves, renames, folder creation, and duplicate cleanup. The instructions require confirmation before deletion, which lowers severity but does not remove filesystem risk.
medium_findings.remediation: Require explicit user approval before moving, renaming, archiving, or deleting files. Prefer dry-run plans and exclude sensitive directories by default.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples Operate on User-Selected Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill includes shell examples using ls, find, file, du, sort, mkdir, and mv against placeholder directories. These commands are not hidden malware, but user-controlled paths and broad scans can expose file metadata or affect many files if used carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and intended to run locally, but they are normal file-management commands. Risk depends on path scope and user approval rather than malicious intent.
medium_findings.remediation: Quote paths, avoid shell interpolation, start with read-only commands, and show the exact command plan before execution.

low_findings.title: MD5 Use Is for Duplicate Detection, Not Security
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography because the skill suggests md5 for exact duplicate detection. In this context md5 is used as a non-security checksum, not for password storage, signatures, authentication, or trust decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text explicitly describes finding duplicate files by hash. I found no evidence that md5 protects secrets or verifies adversarial content.
low_findings.remediation: For higher assurance duplicate checks, use sha256 or compare file sizes before hashing. Do not use md5 for security-sensitive verification.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Flags Are Benign File-Organization Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged reconnaissance terms, but the cited content describes messy folders, naming guidance, and local file cleanup. I found no network scanning, host discovery, credential harvesting, or external endpoint usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious labels do not match the semantic context of the cited lines. The skill contains no URLs, network commands, or instructions to enumerate remote systems.
low_findings.remediation: No security remediation is required for these lines. Keep marketplace warnings focused on local filesystem scope.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad Local File Enumeration and Movement
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill pattern combines local directory enumeration with later move and rename actions. This is useful for organization but dangerous if applied outside an agreed directory or without a dry run.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly enumerates and modifies files. Built-in confirmation guidance reduces abuse risk but still requires careful review.
dangerous_patterns.remediation: Use bounded target directories, require confirmation for each batch action, and maintain an undo log.

content.value_statement: Messy folders make files hard to find and increase duplicate storage. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan local cleanup before changing files.
actual_capabilities: Reviews a target folder and summarizes file types, sizes, dates, and obvious structure problems.
Suggests folder structures based on file type, purpose, project status, or date.
Finds duplicate candidates by file hash, matching names, and similar sizes.
Creates an approval-based cleanup plan before moving, renaming, archiving, or deleting files.
Provides maintenance tips and repeatable commands for future folder cleanup.
limitations: It requires local filesystem access through the host AI tool.
It can move or rename files only after the user approves commands.
Duplicate detection examples use simple command-line checks, not a dedicated forensic tool.
It does not know personal file meaning unless names, paths, or content make context clear.

use_cases.title: Clean a Downloads Folder
use_cases.description: Group documents, images, installers, archives, and uncertain items into clear folders after a review plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare Project Archives
use_cases.description: Separate active work from old project folders and create an archive structure for completed work.

use_cases.title: Reduce Duplicate Storage
use_cases.description: Identify duplicate candidates and compare paths, dates, and names before choosing what to keep.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner cleanup of a single folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Downloads folder. Summarize what is there, identify obvious groups, and propose a cleanup plan. Do not move files yet.

prompt_templates.title: Find Duplicate Candidates
prompt_templates.scenario: Check for duplicate files before deletion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find duplicate candidates in my Documents folder. Show each group with paths, sizes, dates, and a recommendation. Ask before deleting anything.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Project Folder Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Organize workspaces with active and archived projects.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Projects folder. Separate active projects from archive candidates and suggest a consistent folder structure. Include uncertain items for my decision.

prompt_templates.title: Execute Approved Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup after reviewing a plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the approved organization plan for this folder. Create an undo log, run commands in small batches, and stop if any filename conflict appears.

output_examples.input: Organize my Downloads folder without moving anything yet.
output: Current state summary with file counts, large items, and common file types.
Proposed folders for Documents, Images, Installers, Archives, and To-Sort.
A review checklist asking for approval before any file changes.
Duplicate candidate groups with file paths, sizes, and modification dates.
A keep recommendation for each group based on location and recency.
A clear deletion confirmation prompt for each set.

output_examples.input: Clean up my old project folders.
output: Active, archive, template, and uncertain project groups.
Specific move and rename recommendations with reasons.
A batch execution plan that can be approved or modified.

best_practices: Start with one target folder and request a dry-run plan first.
Exclude sensitive, system, backup, and active project folders unless explicitly needed.
Keep an undo log for every move, rename, archive, or deletion action.
anti_patterns: Do not run cleanup across the entire home directory as a first step.
Do not delete duplicate candidates without checking paths, dates, and user intent.
Do not reorganize shared folders without confirming team naming rules and ownership.

faq.question: Can this skill delete files?
faq.answer: It can recommend duplicate deletion, but the instructions require confirmation before deleting anything.

faq.question: Does it upload my files?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md for upload, network requests, or external endpoints.

faq.question: Can it organize photos by date?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill suggests organizing photos by year and month using metadata or file modification dates.

faq.question: Is it safe for important work folders?
faq.answer: Use a dry run first, review the plan, and approve only small batches of changes.

faq.question: How does it find duplicates?
faq.answer: It suggests checking file hashes, matching filenames, similar sizes, paths, and modification dates.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
